title: Zero product property
description: Use the zero product property to solve quadratic equations. For example, solve (x-5)(x+2)=0.
topic_path: Solving and graphing with factored form

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solve for $x$.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$(2x-1)(x+4)=0$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
For any two expressions $A$ and $B$,

>if $A\cdot B=0$ then either $A=0$ or $B=0$.

This is called the *zero product property*.

In our case, $(2x-1)(x+4)=0$. So either $2x-1=0$ or $x+4=0$:

$\begin{align}
(1)&&2x-1&=0
\\\\
&&2x&=1
\\\\
&&x&=\dfrac12
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
(2)&&x+4&=0
\\\\
&&x&=-4
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the zeros of the function.**  
*Enter the solutions from least to greatest.*

$f(x)=(x-5)(5x+2)$

$\text{lesser }x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$\text{greater } x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The **zeros** of function $f$ are the input values that make $f(x)=0$ true.

For any two expressions $A$ and $B$,

if $A\cdot B=0$ then either $A=0$ or $B=0$.

This is called the *zero product property*.

In our case, $(x-5)(5x+2)=0$. So either $x-5=0$ or $5x+2=0$:

$\begin{align}
(1)&&x-5&=0
\\\\
&&x&=5
\end{align}$

$\begin{align}
(2)&&5x+2&=0
\\\\
&&5x&=-2
\\\\
&&x&=-\dfrac25
\end{align}$
